findings.title: Django SECRET_KEY not set
findings.description: SECRET_KEY is missing or empty. This breaks session security and CSRF protection.
findings.suggestion: Set SECRET_KEY to a random 50-character string (use django.core.management.utils.get_random_secret_key())

findings.title: Django ALLOWED_HOSTS is empty
findings.description: ALLOWED_HOSTS is empty or not set. This allows Host header injection attacks.
findings.suggestion: Set ALLOWED_HOSTS to specific domain names, e.g., ["example.com", "www.example.com"]

findings.title: Django SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT is disabled
findings.description: HTTP requests will not be redirected to HTTPS, exposing data to man-in-the-middle attacks.

findings.title: Django SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE is disabled
findings.description: Session cookies will be transmitted over HTTP, exposing them to interception.
findings.suggestion: Set SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=True to transmit session cookies only over HTTPS

findings.title: Django CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE is disabled
findings.description: CSRF tokens will be transmitted over HTTP, exposing them to interception.
findings.suggestion: Set CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE=True to transmit CSRF cookies only over HTTPS

findings.title: Django SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS not configured
findings.description: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) is not enabled. This allows downgrade attacks.
findings.suggestion: Set SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS to at least 31536000 (1 year)

findings.title: Django SECURE_CONTENT_TYPE_NOSNIFF is disabled
findings.description: X-Content-Type-Options header is not set. Browsers may sniff MIME types, enabling XSS attacks.
findings.suggestion: Set SECURE_CONTENT_TYPE_NOSNIFF=True to add X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header
